title: AI Ethics: Right and Wrong
modules: What Makes an AI Decision Ethical?
Who Builds AI and Why It Matters
Bias: When Algorithms Get It Wrong
synopsis: This course introduces everyday people to the moral questions shaping AI development, from bias and privacy to accountability and human rights. No technical background needed — just curiosity about the world AI is building around us.
rationale: With a demand signal of 7/10 and low corpus similarity of 0.61 relative to the nearest course 'AI and Democracy', there is a clear gap for an accessible, ethics-first course that addresses foundational moral reasoning rather than political structures alone.
